# Screenshot placeholders

Replace each dashed SVG with a PNG or WEBP keeping the filename or update Markdown paths accordingly.

Paths look like `/img/placeholders/<name>.svg`.

**Getting started:** `signup-page.svg`, `signup-confirm-email.svg`, `checkout-plan-cards.svg`, `checkout-polar-payment.svg`, `install-verify-email.svg`, `install-business-details.svg`, `install-personalization.svg`, `install-services-templates.svg`, `install-services-edit.svg`, `install-integrations.svg`, `install-payments.svg`, `install-complete.svg`, `install-wizard-progress.svg`, `install-step1-slug-preview.svg`, `install-step2-colors-expanded.svg`, `install-step3-duration-price.svg`.

**Using your workspace Apps overview:** `apps-installed-overview.svg`, `apps-store-browse.svg`, `apps-menu-callouts.svg`, `apps-add-connection.svg`, `apps-post-install-checklist.svg`, `apps-reconnect.svg`, `apps-disconnect-confirm.svg`, `apps-secrets-training.svg`.

**Every App guide (Google Calendar skips generator):** triples `app-<slug>-app-store.svg`, `app-<slug>-connection.svg`, `app-<slug>-in-flow.svg` except Google Calendar filenames `app-google-calendar-app-store.svg`, `connection`, `in-flow`.

App screenshots are authored manually alongside each guide in **`docs/apps/`**; keep filenames aligned with Markdown image paths when you add real PNG or WEBP assets.
